my 2 cents
overall great production for their gacha games
but after playing for a while you will notice many shortcomings 
- Units want dup and their signature but the economy forced you to skip a lot of banners before you think you can pull a unit
- Repititive gameplay (although their minigame events kinda make up for that but still arguable bc not everyone enjoys that)
- HP inflation is pretty bad
- Character designs usually fall in the same description, no actual variety
- Really questionable storytelling and presentation
like if you play casually Hoyo games are still good games

Mihoyo’s gameplan (from players understanding of the company practices), revolvings around giving very little, and so players have more incentive to spend. That is not a good look. Players typically are more incentivised to spend and feel better when there is better trust on this. Leaves a bad taste over time to be supporting the game and be given shit on “anniversaries”. Maybe you’re right about not caring, but then they would not be spending. This becomes a like trust issue and people leave the game. It goes downhill.

People want to progress in a gacha game.
gameplay, story, aesthetics, accessibility, amount of content, update frequency, qc, etc

I can’t exactly pin point it. It wasn’t to destroy player trust. It was mostly horrible gameplay changes and worse monetisation choices. 
Expedition is no longer. Game modes removed. Previous ceo left, story changed. A bit too repetitive (but relatively easy grind).
i really want to get into in again but idk lol
Basically the opposite of mihoyo. More qol, worse events and content.
Content in the sense of being fun.
Now their best efforts to keep game stable is ad revenue and €30 paid skins.
Also less and less game content being made now. Lot of developers allocated to the *next game*. Story release is slowed.

Don’t worry we do have some hard carries in this game. If you get then it’d make general content easier. 
But they themselves may not do well in endgame difficulty, because of enemy inflation and  limited damage on your roster. I think this is a good thing. Damage downtime is very real, so you will have to use control and tank when necessary.
I think this is one game that actually prefers more control in the endgame difficulty rather than dps.
The true experience of picking who to handle without fucking up the entire run.
